          draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\\n865:         for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\\n866:           // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use   }\n982:   }\n983:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n984:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n985:     worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n986:     if workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n994: \n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n997:     callback();\n998:   }\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1000: }\n1001: \n1002: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n1003:                                        const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n1004:                                        std::function<void()> callback) {\n1005:   const auto workers_pending_stop = std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size());\n1006:   for (const auto& Same below inside the lambda.\n659:   stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n660: \n661:   // Tell all workers to stop accepting new connections on this listener.\n662:   draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener\");\n663:   const uint64_t listener_tag = draining_it->listener_->listenerTag();\n664:   stopListener(*draining_it->listener_, {}, [this, listener_tag]() {\n665:     for (auto& listener : draining_listeners_) {\n666:       if (listener.listener_->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n667:         maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener.listener_);\n668:       }\n669:     }\n670:   });\n671: \n672:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n673:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n674:   draining_it->listener_->localDrainManager().startDrainSequence([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n675:     draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"removing draining listener\");\n676:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n677:       // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n678:       // to remove the listener.\n679:       worker->removeListener(*draining_it->listener_, [this, draining_it]() -> void {\n680:         // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to the\n681:         // main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the listener\n682:         // while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n683:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n684:           if (--draining_it->workers_pending_removal_ == 0) {\n685:             draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener removal complete\");\n686:             draining_listeners_.erase(draining_it);\n687:             stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n688:           }\n689:         });\n690:       });\n691:     }\n692:   });\n693: \n694:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n695: }\n696: \n697: ListenerManagerImpl::ListenerList::iterator\n698: ListenerManagerImpl::getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name) {\n699:   auto ret = listeners.end();\n700:   for (auto it = listeners.begin(); it != listeners.end(); ++it) {\n701:     if ((*it)->name() == name) {\n702:       // There should only ever be a single listener per name in the list. }\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>> ret;\n714: \n715:   size_t size = 0;\n716:   size += state & WARMING ? warming_listeners_.size() : 0;\n717:   size += state & ACTIVE ? active_listeners_.size() : 0;\n718:   size += state & DRAINING ? draining_listeners_.size() : 0;\n719:   ret.reserve(size);\n720: \n721:   if (state & WARMING) {\n722:     for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n723:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n724:     }\n725:   }\n726:   if (state & ACTIVE) {\n727:     for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n728:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n729:     }\n730:   }\n731:   if (state & DRAINING) {\n732:     for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n733:       ret.push_back(*(draining_listener.listener_));\n734:     }\n735:   }\n736:   return ret;\n737: }\n738: \n739: bool ListenerManagerImpl::doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n740:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n741:     for (auto& socket_factory : listener.listenSocketFactories()) {\n742:       absl::Status success = (socket_factory->doFinalPreWorkerInit());\n743:       if (!success.ok()) {\n744:         ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\",\n745:                   listener.name(), success.message());\n746:         return false;\n747:       }\n748:     }\n749:     return true;\n750:   }\n751:   END_TRY\n752:   CATCH(EnvoyException & e, {\n753:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\", listener.name(),\n754:               e.what());\n755:     return false;\n756:   });\n757: }\n758: \n759: void ListenerManagerImpl::addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker,\n760:                                               absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n761:                                               ListenerImpl& listener,\n762:                                               ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback) {\n763:   if (overridden_listener.has_value()) {\n764:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"replacing existing listener {}\", overridden_listener.value());\n765:   }\n766:   worker.addListener(\n767:       overridden_listener, listener,\n768:       [this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n769:         // The add listener completion runs on the worker thread. Post back to the main thread to\n770:         // avoid locking.\n771:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n772:           stats_.listener_create_success_.inc();\n773:           if (completion_callback) {\n774:             completion_callback();\n775:           }\n776:         });\n777:       },\n778:       server_.runtime(), server_.api().randomGenerator());\n779: }\n780: \n781: void ListenerManagerImpl::onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n782:   // The warmed listener should be added first so that the worker will accept new connections\n783:   // when it stops listening on the old listener.\n784:   if (!doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(listener)) {\n785:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n786:     // TODO(mattklein123): Technically we don't need to remove the active listener if one exists.\n787:     // The following call will remove both.\n788:     removeListenerInternal(listener.name(), true);\n789:     return;\n790:   }\n791:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n792:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, listener, nullptr);\n793:   }\n794: \n795:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n796:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n797: \n798:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"warm complete. updating active listener\");\n799:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n800:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n801:     // state.\n802:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n803:     *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n804:     drainListener(std::move(listener));\n805:   } else {\n806:     active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(*existing_warming_listener));\n807:   }\n808: \n809:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n810:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n811: }\n812: \n813: void ListenerManagerImpl::inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n814:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n815:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n816:   ASSERT(existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end());\n817:   ASSERT(*existing_warming_listener != nullptr);\n818: \n819:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"execute in place filter chain update\");\n820: \n821:   // Now that in place filter chain update was decided, the replaced listener must be in active\n822:   // list. It requires stop/remove listener procedure cancelling the in placed update if any.\n823:   ASSERT(existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end());\n824:   ASSERT(*existing_active_listener != nullptr);\n825: \n826:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n827:     // Explicitly override the existing listener with a new listener config.\n828:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, listener.listenerTag(), listener, nullptr);\n829:   }\n830: \n831:   auto previous_listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n832:   *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n833:   // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainFilterChains` as it depends on\n834:   // their state.\n835:   drainFilterChains(std::move(previous_listener), **existing_active_listener);\n836: \n837:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n838:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n839: }\n840: \n841: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n842:                                             ListenerImpl& new_listener) {\n843:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n844:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager>::iterator draining_group =\n845:       draining_filter_chains_manager_.emplace(draining_filter_chains_manager_.begin(),\n846:                                               std::move(draining_listener), workers_.size());\n847:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().diffFilterChain(\n848:       new_listener, [&draining_group](Network::DrainableFilterChain& filter_chain) mutable {\n849:         filter_chain.startDraining();\n850:         draining_group->addFilterChainToDrain(filter_chain);\n851:       });\n852:   auto filter_chain_size = draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains();\n853:   stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.add(filter_chain_size);\n854:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n855:       absl::StrCat(\"draining \", filter_chain_size, \" filter chains in listener \",\n856:                    draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n857: \n858:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n859:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n860:   draining_group->startDrainSequence(\n861:       server_.options().drainTime(), server_.dispatcher(), [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n862:         draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n863:             absl::StrCat(\"removing draining filter chains from listener \",\n864:                          draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n865:         for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n866:           // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n867:           // to remove the filter chains.\n868:           worker->removeFilterChains(\n869:               draining_group->getDrainingListenerTag(), draining_group->getDrainingFilterChains(),\n870:               [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n871:                 // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to\n872:                 // the main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the\n873:                 // listener while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n874:                 server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_group]() -> void {\n875:                   if (draining_group->decWorkersPendingRemoval() == 0) {\n876:                     draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n877:                         absl::StrCat(\"draining filter chains from listener \",\n878:                                      draining_group->getDrainingListener().name(), \" complete\"));\n879:                     stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.sub(\n880:                         draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains());\n881:                     draining_filter_chains_manager_.erase(draining_group);\n882:                   }\n883:                 });\n884:               });\n885:         }\n886:       });\n887:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n888: }\n889: \n890: uint64_t ListenerManagerImpl::numConnections() const {\n891:   uint64_t num_connections = 0;\n892:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n893:     num_connections += worker->numConnections();\n894:   }\n895: \n896:   return num_connections;\n897: }\n898: \n899: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListener(const std::string& name) {\n900:   return removeListenerInternal(name, true);\n901: }\n902: \n903: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListenerInternal(const std::string& name,\n904:                                                  bool dynamic_listeners_only) {\n905:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin remove listener: name={}\", name);\n906: \n907:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n908:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n909:   if ((existing_warming_listener == warming_listeners_.end() ||\n910:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_warming_listener)->blockRemove())) &&\n911:       (existing_active_listener == active_listeners_.end() ||\n912:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_active_listener)->blockRemove()))) {\n913:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"unknown/locked listener '{}'. no remove\", name);\n914:     return false;\n915:   }\n916: \n917:   // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n918:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n919:     (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n920:     warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n921:   }\n922: \n923:   // If there is an active listener it needs to be moved to draining after workers have started, or\n924:   // destroyed directly.\n925:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n926:     // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\n927:     // listeners only after this occurs.\n928:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n929:     // state.\n930:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n931:     active_listeners_.erase(existing_active_listener);\n932:     if (workers_started_) {\n933:       drainListener(std::move(listener));\n934:     }\n935:   }\n936: \n937:   stats_.listener_removed_.inc();\n938:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n939:   return true;\n940: }\n941: \n942: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog,\n943:                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\n944:   ENVOY_LOG(info, \"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\");\n945:   ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\n951:     workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\n952:   };\n953: \n954:   // We can not use \"Cleanup\" to simplify this logic here, because it results in a issue if Envoy is\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\n961:   for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\n962:     auto& listener = *listener_it;\n963:     listener_it++;\n964: \n965:     absl::StatusOr<bool> init_status = doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(*listener);\n966:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(init_status.status());\n967: \n968:     if (!*init_status) {\n969:       incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n970:       removeListenerInternal(listener->name(), false);\n971:       continue;\n972:     }\n973:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n974:       addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, *listener,\n975:                           [this, listeners_pending_init, callback]() {\n976:                             if (--(*listeners_pending_init) == 0) {\n977:                               stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n978:                               callback();\n979:                             }\n980:                           });\n981:     }\n982:   }\n983:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n984:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n985:     worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n986:     if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\n987:       worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\n988:     }\n989:     i++;\n990:   }\n991: \n992:   // Wait for workers to start running.\n993:   workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n994: \n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n997:     callback();\n998:   }\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1000: }\n1001: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc%3FL1-1000"}}}

ProdListenerComponentFactory::createListenSocket(\n    Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address, Network::Socket::Type socket_type,\n    const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options, BindType bind_type,\n    const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t worker_index) {\n  ASSERT(socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream ||\n         socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Datagram);\n\n  // First we try to get the socket from our parent if applicable in each case below.\n  if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::Pipe) {\n    if (socket_type != Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n      // This could be implemented in the future, since Unix domain sockets\n      // support SOCK_DGRAM, but there would need to be a way to specify it in\n      // envoy.api.v2.core.Pipe.\n      return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n          fmt::format(\"socket type {} not supported for pipes\", toString(socket_type)));\n    }\n    const std::string addr = fmt::format(\"unix://{}\", address->asString());\n    const int fd = server_.hotRestart().duplicateParentListenSocket(addr, worker_index);\n    Network::IoHandlePtr io_handle = std::make_unique<Network::IoSocketHandleImpl>(fd);\n    if (io_handle->isOpen()) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"obtained socket for address {} from parent\", addr);\n      return std::make_shared<Network::UdsListenSocket>(std::move(io_handle), address);\n    }\n    return std::make_shared<Network::UdsListenSocket>(address);\n  } else if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::EnvoyInternal) {\n    // Listener manager should have validated that envoy internal address doesn't work with udp\n    // listener yet.\n    ASSERT(socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream);\n    return std::make_shared<Network::InternalListenSocket>(address);\n  }\n\n  const std::string scheme = (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream)\n address->asString());\n\n  if (bind_type != BindType::NoBind) {\n    const int fd = server_.hotRestart().duplicateParentListenSocket(addr, worker_index);\n    if (fd != -1) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"obtained socket for address {} from parent\", addr);\n      Network::IoHandlePtr io_handle = std::make_unique<Network::IoSocketHandleImpl>(fd);\n      if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n        return std::make_shared<Network::TcpListenSocket>(std::move(io_handle), address, options);\n      } else {\n        auto socket = std::make_shared<Network::UdpListenSocket>(\n            std::move(io_handle), address, options,\n            server_.hotRestart().parentDrainedCallbackRegistrar());\n        return socket;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n    return std::make_shared<Network::TcpListenSocket>(\n        address, options, bind_type != BindType::NoBind, creation_options);\n  } else {\n    return std::make_shared<Network::UdpListenSocket>(\n        address, options, bind_type != BindType::NoBind, creation_options);\n  }\n}\n\nDrainManagerPtr DynamicListener*> listener_map;\n\n  for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n    if (!name_matcher.match(listener->config().name())) {\n      continue;\n    }\n    if POOL_GAUGE(scope))};\n}\n\nabsl::StatusOr<bool>\nListenerOrchestrator::addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n                                          const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api) {\n  std::string name;\n  if (!config.name().empty()) {\n    name = config.name();\n  } else {\n    // TODO (soulxu): The random uuid name is bad for logging. We can use listening addresses in\n    // the log to improve that.\n    name = server_.api().randomGenerator().uuid();\n  }\n\n  // TODO(junr03): currently only one ApiListener can be installed via bootstrap to avoid having to\n  // build a collection of listeners, and to have to be able to warm and drain the listeners. In the\n  // future allow multiple ApiListeners, and allow them to be created via LDS as well as bootstrap.\n  if (config.has_api_listener()) {\n    if (config.has_internal_listener()) {\n      return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n          \"error adding listener named '{}': api_listener and internal_listener cannot be both set\",\n          name));\n    }\n    if (!api_listener_ && !added_via_api) {\n      auto listener_or_error = HttpApiListener::create(config, server_, config.name());\n      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n      api_listener_ = std::move(listener_or_error.value());\n      return true;\n    } else {\n      ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"listener {} can not be added because currently only one ApiListener is \"\n                      \"allowed, and it can only be added via bootstrap configuration\");\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Address field is not required for internal listeners.\n  if (!config.has_internal_listener() && !config.has_address()) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n        fmt::format(\"error adding listener named '{}': address is necessary\", name));\n  }\n\n  auto it = error_state_tracker_.find(name);\n  absl::StatusOr<bool> add_or_update_status;\n  TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n    add_or_update_status = addOrUpdateListenerInternal(config, version_info, added_via_api, name);\n  }\n  END_TRY\n  CATCH(const EnvoyException& e, { add_or_update_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(e.what()); })\n  if (!add_or_update_status.status().ok()) {\n    if (it == error_state_tracker_.end()) {\n      it = error_state_tracker_.emplace(name, std::make_unique<UpdateFailureState>()).first;\n    }\n    TimestampUtil::systemClockToTimestamp(server_.api().timeSource().systemTime(),\n                                          *(it->second->mutable_last_update_attempt()));\n    it->second->set_details(add_or_update_status.status().message());\n    it->second->mutable_failed_configuration()->PackFrom(config);\n  }\n  return add_or_update_status;\n}\n\nabsl::Status\nListenerOrchestrator::setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n                                                    const ListenerImpl& existing_listener) {\n  bool same_socket_options = true;\n  if (new_listener.reusePort() != existing_listener.reusePort()) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n        \"Listener {}: reuse port cannot be changed during an update\", new_listener.name()));\n  }\n\n  same_socket_options = existing_listener.socketOptionsEqual(new_listener);\n  if (!same_socket_options && new_listener.reusePort() == false) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n        fmt::format(\"Listener {}: doesn't support update any socket options \"\n                    \"when the reuse port isn't enabled\",\n                    new_listener.name()));\n  }\n\n  if (!(existing_listener.hasCompatibleAddress(new_listener) && same_socket_options)) {\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(new_listener.name(), new_listener));\n  } else {\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(new_listener.cloneSocketFactoryFrom(existing_listener));\n  }\n  return absl::OkStatus();\n}\n\nabsl::StatusOr<bool> ListenerOrchestrator::addOrUpdateListenerInternal(\n    const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\n    bool added_via_api, const std::string& name) {\n\n  if (listenersStopped(config)) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(\n        debug,\n        \"listener {} can not be added because listeners in the traffic direction {} are stopped\",\n        name, envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection_Name(config.traffic_direction()));\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  const uint64_t hash = MessageUtil::hash(config);\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin add/update listener: name={} hash={}\", name, hash);\n\n  auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n  auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n\n  // The listener should be updated back to its original state and the warming listener should be\n  // removed.\n  if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end() &&\n      existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n      (*existing_active_listener)->blockUpdate(hash)) {\n    warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n    updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n    stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Do a quick blocked update check before going further. This check needs to be done against both\n  // warming and active.\n  if ((existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end() &&\n       (*existing_warming_listener)->blockUpdate(hash)) ||\n      (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n       (*existing_active_listener)->blockUpdate(hash))) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"duplicate/locked listener '{}'. no add/update\", name);\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  ListenerImplPtr new_listener = nullptr;\n\n  // In place filter chain update depends on the active listener at worker.\n  if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n      (*existing_active_listener)->supportUpdateFilterChain(config, workers_started_)) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use in place update filter chain update path for listener name={} hash={}\",\n              name, hash);\n    auto listener_or_error =\n        (*existing_active_listener)->newListenerWithFilterChain(config, workers_started_, hash);\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n    new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n    stats_.listener_in_place_updated_.inc();\n  } else {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use full listener update path for listener name={} hash={}\", name, hash);\n    auto listener_or_error = ListenerImpl::create(config, version_info, *this, name, added_via_api,\n                                                  workers_started_, hash);\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n    new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n  }\n\n  ListenerImpl& new_listener_ref = *new_listener;\n\n  bool added = false;\n  if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n    ASSERT(workers_started_);\n    new_listener->debugLog(\"update warming listener\");\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_warming_listener));\n    // In this case we can just replace inline.\n    *existing_warming_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n  } else if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_active_listener));\n    // In this case we have no warming listener, so what we do depends on whether workers\n    // have been started or not.\n    if (workers_started_) {\n      new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n      warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n    } else {\n      new_listener->debugLog(\"update active listener\");\n      *existing_active_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n    }\n  } else {\n    // We have no warming or active listener so we need to make a new one. What we do depends on\n    // whether workers have been started or not.\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(name, *new_listener));\n    if (workers_started_) {\n      new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n      warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n    } else {\n      new_listener->debugLog(\"add active listener\");\n      active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n    }\n\n    added = true;\n  }\n\n  updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n  if (added) {\n    stats_.listener_added_.inc();\n  } else {\n    stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n  }\n\n  new_listener_ref.initialize();\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool ListenerOrchestrator::hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n                                                            const ListenerImpl& listener) {\n  for (const auto& existing_listener : list) {\n    if (existing_listener->hasDuplicatedAddress(listener)) {\n      return true;\n    }\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener) {\n  // First add the listener to the draining list.\n  std::list<DrainingListener>::iterator draining_it = draining_listeners_.emplace(\n      draining_listeners_.begin(), std::move(listener), workers_.size());\n\n  // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n  // restart. Same below inside the lambda.\n  stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n\n  // Tell all workers to stop accepting new connections on this listener.\n  draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener\");\n  const uint64_t listener_tag = draining_it->listener_->listenerTag();\n  stopListener(*draining_it->listener_, {}, [this, listener_tag]() {\n    for (auto& listener : draining_listeners_) {\n      if (listener.listener_->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n        maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener.listener_);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n  // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n  draining_it->listener_->localDrainManager().startDrainSequence([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n    draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"removing draining listener\");\n    for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n      // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n      // to remove the listener.\n      worker->removeListener(*draining_it->listener_, [this, draining_it]() -> void {\n        // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to the\n        // main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the listener\n        // while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n        server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n          if (--draining_it->workers_pending_removal_ == 0) {\n            draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener removal complete\");\n            draining_listeners_.erase(draining_it);\n            stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n          }\n        });\n      });\n    }\n  });\n\n  updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n}\n\nListenerOrchestrator::ListenerList::iterator\nListenerOrchestrator::getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name) {\n  auto ret = listeners.end();\n  for (auto it = listeners.begin(); it != listeners.end(); ++it) {\n    if ((*it)->name() == name) {\n      // There should only ever be a single listener per name in the list. We could return faster\n      // but take the opportunity to assert that fact.\n      ASSERT(ret == listeners.end());\n      ret = it;\n    }\n  }\n  return ret;\n}\n\nstd::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\nListenerOrchestrator::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n  std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>> ret;\n\n  size_t size = 0;\n  size += state & WARMING ? warming_listeners_.size() : 0;\n  size += state & ACTIVE ? active_listeners_.size() : 0;\n  size += state & DRAINING ? draining_listeners_.size() : 0;\n  ret.reserve(size);\n\n  if (state & WARMING) {\n    for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n      ret.push_back(*listener);\n    }\n  }\n  if (state & ACTIVE) {\n    for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n      ret.push_back(*listener);\n    }\n  }\n  if (state & DRAINING) {\n    for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n      ret.push_back(*(draining_listener.listener_));\n    }\n  }\n  return ret;\n}\n\nbool ListenerOrchestrator::doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n  TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n    for (auto& socket_factory : listener.listenSocketFactories()) {\n      absl::Status success = (socket_factory->doFinalPreWorkerInit());\n      if (!success.ok()) {\n        ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\",\n                  listener.name(), success.message());\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n    return true;\n  }\n  END_TRY\n  CATCH(EnvoyException & e, {\n    ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\", listener.name(),\n              e.what());\n    return false;\n  });\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker,\n                                               absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n                                               ListenerImpl& listener,\n                                               ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback) {\n  if (overridden_listener.has_value()) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"replacing existing listener {}\", overridden_listener.value());\n  }\n  worker.addListener(\n      overridden_listener, listener,\n      [this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n        // The add listener completion runs on the worker thread. Post back to the main thread to\n        // avoid locking.\n        server_.dispatcher().post([this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n          stats_.listener_create_success_.inc();\n          if (completion_callback) {\n            completion_callback();\n          }\n        });\n      },\n      server_.runtime(), server_.api().randomGenerator());\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n  // The warmed listener should be added first so that the worker will accept new connections\n  // when it stops listening on the old listener.\n  if (!doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(listener)) {\n    incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n    // TODO(mattklein123): Technically we don't need to remove the active listener if one exists.\n    // The following call will remove both.\n    removeListenerInternal(listener.name(), true);\n    return;\n  }\n  for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n    addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, listener, nullptr);\n  }\n\n  auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n  auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n\n  (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"warm complete. updating active listener\");\n  if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n    // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n    // state.\n    auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n    *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n    drainListener(std::move(listener));\n  } else {\n    active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(*existing_warming_listener));\n  }\n\n  warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n  updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n  auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n  auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n  ASSERT(existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end());\n  ASSERT(*existing_warming_listener != nullptr);\n\n  (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"execute in place filter chain update\");\n\n  // Now that in place filter chain update was decided, the replaced listener must be in active\n  // list. It requires stop/remove listener procedure cancelling the in placed update if any.\n  ASSERT(existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end());\n  ASSERT(*existing_active_listener != nullptr);\n\n  for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n    // Explicitly override the existing listener with a new listener config.\n    addListenerToWorker(*worker, listener.listenerTag(), listener, nullptr);\n  }\n\n  auto previous_listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n  *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n  // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainFilterChains` as it depends on\n  // their state.\n  drainFilterChains(std::move(previous_listener), **existing_active_listener);\n\n  warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n  updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n                                             ListenerImpl& new_listener) {\n  // First add the listener to the draining list.\n  std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager>::iterator draining_group =\n      draining_filter_chains_manager_.emplace(draining_filter_chains_manager_.begin(),\n                                              std::move(draining_listener), workers_.size());\n  draining_group->getDrainingListener().diffFilterChain(\n      new_listener, [&draining_group](Network::DrainableFilterChain& filter_chain) mutable {\n        filter_chain.startDraining();\n        draining_group->addFilterChainToDrain(filter_chain);\n      });\n  auto filter_chain_size = draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains();\n  stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.add(filter_chain_size);\n  draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n      absl::StrCat(\"draining \", filter_chain_size, \" filter chains in listener \",\n                   draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n\n  // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n  // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n  draining_group->startDrainSequence(\n      server_.options().drainTime(), server_.dispatcher(), [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n        draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n            absl::StrCat(\"removing draining filter chains from listener \",\n                         draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n        for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n          // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n          // to remove the filter chains.\n          worker->removeFilterChains(\n              draining_group->getDrainingListenerTag(), draining_group->getDrainingFilterChains(),\n              [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n                // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to\n                // the main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the\n                // listener while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n                server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_group]() -> void {\n                  if (draining_group->decWorkersPendingRemoval() == 0) {\n                    draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n                        absl::StrCat(\"draining filter chains from listener \",\n                                     draining_group->getDrainingListener().name(), \" complete\"));\n                    stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.sub(\n                        draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains());\n                    draining_filter_chains_manager_.erase(draining_group);\n                  }\n                });\n              });\n        }\n      });\n  updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n}\n\nuint64_t ListenerOrchestrator::numConnections() const {\n  uint64_t num_connections = 0;\n  for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n    num_connections += worker->numConnections();\n  }\n\n  return num_connections;\n}\n\nbool ListenerOrchestrator::removeListener(const std::string& name) {\n  return removeListenerInternal(name, true);\n}\n\nbool ListenerOrchestrator::removeListenerInternal(const std::string& name,\n                                                  bool dynamic_listeners_only) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin remove listener: name={}\", name);\n\n  auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n  auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n  if ((existing_warming_listener == warming_listeners_.end() ||\n       (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_warming_listener)->blockRemove())) &&\n      (existing_active_listener == active_listeners_.end() ||\n       (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_active_listener)->blockRemove()))) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"unknown/locked listener '{}'. no remove\", name);\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n  if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n    (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n    warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n  }\n\n  // If there is an active listener it needs to be moved to draining after workers have started, or\n  // destroyed directly.\n  if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n    // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\n    // listeners only after this occurs.\n    // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n    // state.\n    auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n    active_listeners_.erase(existing_active_listener);\n    if (workers_started_) {\n      drainListener(std::move(listener));\n    }\n  }\n\n  stats_.listener_removed_.inc();\n  updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n  return true;\n}\n\nabsl::Status ListenerOrchestrator::startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog,\n                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(info, \"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\");\n  ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n  workers_started_ = true;\n  uint32_t i = 0;\n\n  absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n  std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\n    workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\n  };\n\n  // We can not use \"Cleanup\" to simplify this logic here, because it results in a issue if Envoy is\n  // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n  // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n  const auto listeners_pending_init =\n      std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n  ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n  // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\n  for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\n    auto& listener = *listener_it;\n    listener_it++;\n\n    absl::StatusOr<bool> init_status = doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(*listener);\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(init_status.status());\n\n    if (!*init_status) {\n      incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n      removeListenerInternal(listener->name(), false);\n      continue;\n    }\n    for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n      addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, *listener,\n                          [this, listeners_pending_init, callback]() {\n                            if (--(*listeners_pending_init) == 0) {\n                              stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n                              callback();\n                            }\n                          });\n    }\n  }\n  for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n    worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n    if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\n      worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\n    }\n    i++;\n  }\n\n  // Wait for workers to start running.\n  workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n\n  if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n    stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n    callback();\n  }\n  return absl::OkStatus();\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n                                        const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n                                        std::function<void()> callback) {\n  const auto workers_pending_stop = std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size());\n  for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n    worker->stopListener(listener, options, [this, callback, workers_pending_stop]() {\n      if (--(*workers_pending_stop) == 0) {\n        server_.dispatcher().post(callback);\n      }\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\n                                         const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) {\n  stop_listeners_type_ = stop_listeners_type;\n  for (Network::ListenerConfig& listener : listeners()) {\n    if (stop_listeners_type != StopListenersType::InboundOnly ||\n        listener.listenerInfo()->direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin stop listener: name={}\", listener.name());\n      auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n      // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n      if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n        (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n        warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n      }\n      // Close the socket once all workers stopped accepting its connections.\n      // This allows clients to fast fail instead of waiting in the accept queue.\n      const uint64_t listener_tag = listener.listenerTag();\n      stopListener(listener, options, [this, listener_tag]() {\n        stats_.listener_stopped_.inc();\n        for (auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n          if (listener->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n            maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener);\n          }\n        }\n      });\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::stopWorkers() {\n  if (!workers_started_) {\n    return;\n  }\n  for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n    worker->stop();\n  }\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_states) {\n  overall_error_state_ = std::move(failure_states);\n}\n\nListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\n    ListenerImpl& listener,\n    Server::Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context)\n    : listener_(listener), validator_(listener.validation_visitor_),\n      listener_component_factory_(*listener.parent_.factory_), factory_context_(factory_context) {}\n\nabsl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\nListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChain(\n    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n    FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const {\n  return buildFilterChainInternal(filter_chain,\n                                  context_creator.createFilterChainFactoryContext(&filter_chain));\n}\n\nabsl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\nListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChainInternal(\n    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n    Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const {\n  // If the cluster doesn't have transport socket configured, then use the default \"raw_buffer\"\n  // transport socket or BoringSSL-based \"tls\" transport socket if TLS settings are configured.\n  // We copy by value first then override if necessary.\n  auto transport_socket = filter_chain.transport_socket();\n  if (!filter_chain.has_transport_socket()) {\n    envoy::extensions::transport_sockets::raw_buffer::v3::RawBuffer raw_buffer;\n    transport_socket.mutable_typed_config()->PackFrom(raw_buffer);\n    transport_socket.set_name(\"envoy.transport_sockets.raw_buffer\");\n  }\n\n  auto& config_factory = Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<\n      Server::Configuration::DownstreamTransportSocketConfigFactory>(transport_socket);\n  // The only connection oriented UDP transport protocol right now is QUIC.\n  const bool is_quic =\n      listener_.udpListenerConfig().has_value() &&\n      !listener_.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless();\n#if defined(ENVOY_ENABLE_QUIC)\n  if (is_quic &&\n      dynamic_cast<Quic::QuicServerTransportSocketConfigFactory*>(&config_factory) == nullptr) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n        fmt::format(\"error building filter chain for quic listener: wrong \"\n                    \"transport socket config specified for quic transport socket: \"\n                    \"{}. \\nUse QuicDownstreamTransport instead.\",\n                    transport_socket.DebugString()));\n  }\n  const std::string hcm_str =\n      \"type.googleapis.com/\"\n      \"envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager\";\n  if (is_quic &&\n      (filter_chain.filters().empty() ||\n       filter_chain.filters(filter_chain.filters().size() - 1).typed_config().type_url() !=\n           hcm_str)) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n        fmt::format(\"error building network filter chain for quic listener: requires \"\n                    \"http_connection_manager filter to be last in the chain.\"));\n  }\n#else\n  // When QUIC is compiled out it should not be possible to configure either the QUIC transport\n  // socket or the QUIC listener and get to this point.\n  ASSERT(!is_quic);\n#endif\n  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr message =\n      Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(transport_socket, validator_, config_factory);\n\n  std::vector<std::string> server_names(filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().begin(),\n                                        filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().end());\n\n  auto factory_or_error = config_factory.createTransportSocketFactory(*message, factory_context_,\n                                                                      std::move(server_names));\n  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_or_error.status());\n  auto factory_list_or_error = listener_component_factory_.createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\n      filter_chain.filters(), *filter_chain_factory_context);\n  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_list_or_error.status());\n\n  auto filter_chain_res = std::make_shared<FilterChainImpl>(\n      std::move(factory_or_error.value()), std::move(*factory_list_or_error),\n      std::chrono::milliseconds(\n          PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(filter_chain, transport_socket_connect_timeout, 0)),\n      filter_chain.name());\n\n  filter_chain_res->setFilterChainFactoryContext(std::move(filter_chain_factory_context));\n  return filter_chain_res;\n}\n\nabsl::Status ListenerOrchestrator::setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name,\n                                                                 ListenerImpl& listener) {\n  if (hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(warming_listeners_, listener) ||\n      hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(active_listeners_, listener)) {\n    const std::string message =\n        fmt::format(\"error adding listener: '{}' has duplicate address '{}' as existing listener\",\n                    name, absl::StrJoin(listener.addresses(), \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()));\n    ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"{}\", message);\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\n  }\n\n  // Search through draining listeners to see if there is a listener that has a socket factory for\n  // the same address we are configured for. This is an edge case, but\n  // may happen if a listener is removed and then added back with a same or different name and\n  // intended to listen on the same address. This should work and not fail.\n  const ListenerImpl* draining_listener_ptr = nullptr;\n  auto existing_draining_listener =\n      std::find_if(draining_listeners_.cbegin(), draining_listeners_.cend(),\n                   [&listener](const DrainingListener& draining_listener) {\n                     return draining_listener.listener_->listenSocketFactories()[0]\n                                ->getListenSocket(0)\n                                ->isOpen() &&\n                            listener.hasCompatibleAddress(*draining_listener.listener_);\n                   });\n\n  if (existing_draining_listener != draining_listeners_.cend()) {\n    existing_draining_listener->listener_->debugLog(\"clones listener sockets\");\n    draining_listener_ptr = existing_draining_listener->listener_.get();\n  } else {\n    auto existing_draining_filter_chain = std::find_if(\n        draining_filter_chains_manager_.cbegin(), draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend(),\n        [&listener](const DrainingFilterChainsManager& draining_filter_chain) {\n          return draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener()\n                     .listenSocketFactories()[0]\n                     ->getListenSocket(0)\n                     ->isOpen() &&\n                 listener.hasCompatibleAddress(draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener());\n        });\n\n    if (existing_draining_filter_chain != draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend()) {\n      existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\"clones listener socket\");\n      draining_listener_ptr = &existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener();\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (draining_listener_ptr != nullptr) {\n    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(listener.cloneSocketFactoryFrom(*draining_listener_ptr));\n  } else {\n    return createListenSocketFactory(listener);\n  }\n  return absl::OkStatus();\n}\n\nabsl::Status ListenerOrchestrator::createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n  Network::Socket::Type socket_type = listener.socketType();\n  ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoBind;\n  if (listener.bindToPort()) {\n    bind_type = listener.reusePort() ? ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort\n                                     : ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoReusePort;\n  }\n  absl::Status socket_status = absl::OkStatus();\n  TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n    Network::SocketCreationOptions creation_options;\n    creation_options.mptcp_enabled_ = listener.mptcpEnabled();\n    for (std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>::size_type i = 0;\n         i < listener.addresses().size(); i++) {\n      auto factory_or_error = ListenSocketFactoryImpl::create(\n          *factory_, listener.addresses()[i], socket_type, listener.listenSocketOptions(i),\n          listener.name(), listener.tcpBacklogSize(), bind_type, creation_options,\n          server_.options().concurrency());\n      if (!factory_or_error.status().ok()) {\n        socket_status = factory_or_error.status();\n      } else {\n        socket_status = listener.addSocketFactory(std::move(*factory_or_error));\n      }\n      if (!socket_status.ok()) {\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  END_TRY\n  CATCH(const EnvoyException& e, {\n    socket_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(e.what());\n    ;\n  });\n  if (!socket_status.ok()) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(error, \"listener '{}' failed to bind or apply socket options: {}\", listener.name(),\n              socket_status.message());\n    incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n  }\n  return socket_status;\n}\n\nvoid ListenerOrchestrator::maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n  if (!listener.udpListenerConfig().has_value() ||\n      listener.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless()) {\n    // Close the listen sockets right away to avoid leaving TCP connections in accept queue\n    // already waiting for long timeout. However, connection-oriented UDP listeners shouldn't\n    // close the socket because they need to receive packets for existing connections via the\n    // listen sockets.\n    listener.closeAllSockets();\n\n    // In case of this listener was in-place updated previously and in the filter chains draining\n    // procedure, so close the sockets for the previous draining listener.\n    for (auto& manager : draining_filter_chains_manager_) {\n      // A listener can be in-place updated multiple times, so there may\n      // have multiple draining listeners with same tag.\n      if (manager.getDrainingListenerTag() == listener.listenerTag()) {\n        manager.getDrainingListener().closeAllSockets();\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nApiListenerOptRef ListenerOrchestrator::apiListener() {\n  return api_listener_ ? ApiListenerOptRef(std::ref(*api_listener_)) : absl::nullopt;\n}\n\nREGISTER_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl, ListenerManagerFactory);\n\n} // namespace Server\n} // namespace Envoy\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1178,"cache_read_input_tokens":78743,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1178},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012dvL7KdCqnsg4xeEynjJ3F","session_id":"ad993b7d-d38f-4cff-844d-73e3581e179e","uuid":"12f62966-d084-4571-b70c-9de9328c8dcd"}

This makes sure that during listener\\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\\n155:     //\\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\\n158:     // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\\n159:     // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\\n160:     // probably not worth the difficulty.)\\n161:     sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\\n162:   }\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\\n166:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\\n167:   // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\\n168:   // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. 1; i < num_sockets; i++) {\n130:     if (bind_type_ != ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort && sockets_[0] != nullptr) {\n131:       sockets_.push_back(sockets_[0]->duplicate());\n132:     } ListenSocketFactoryImpl& factory_to_clone)\n142:     : factory_(factory_to_clone.factory_), local_address_(factory_to_clone.local_address_),\n143:       socket_type_(factory_to_clone.socket_type_), options_(factory_to_clone.options_),\n144:       listener_name_(factory_to_clone.listener_name_),\n145:       tcp_backlog_size_(factory_to_clone.tcp_backlog_size_),\n146:       bind_type_(factory_to_clone.bind_type_),\n147:       socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\n148:   for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\n149:     // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\n155:     //\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\n158:     // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\n159:     // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\n160:     // probably not worth the difficulty.)\n161:     sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\n162:   }\n163: }\n164: \n165: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\n166:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\n167:   // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\n168:   // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01QDhPRY8oYXASGe3Y5gxeLm","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01SZPtQ5gN9hd4z9KaQLRpt4","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport os\n\ncontent = \"\"\"# Listener implementation\n\n## Socket handling\n\nEnvoy uses the following procedure for creating sockets and assigning them to workers.\n\n1. When a listener is created, a socket is pre-created for every worker on the main thread. This\n   allows most errors to be caught early on in the listener creation process (e.g., bad socket\n   option, unable to bind, etc.).\n    * If using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for every worker.\n    * If not using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for worker 0, and then that socket\n      is duplicated for all other workers.\n2. In the case of a listener update in which the address/socket is to be shared (i.e., a named\n   listener that has configuration settings changed but listens on the same address will share\n   sockets with the draining listener), all sockets are duplicated regardless of whether using\n   reuse_port or not. This keeps the logic equivalent, and also makes sure that when using\n   reuse_port no TCP connections are dropped during the drain process (in the race condition between\n   assigning a queue and closing the listening socket on the old listener).\n3. In the case of hot restart, all sockets are requested by worker index. If the old process has\n   a match, the socket is duplicated and sent to the new process. Thus, hot restart and a listener\n   update effectively share the same update process. This is so that no connections are dropped\n   when using reuse_port.\n4. A consequence of every listener having dedicated sockets (whether duplicated or not) is that\n   a listener can close() its sockets when removed without concern for other listeners.\n\nFor more information, see `ListenSocketFactoryImpl` and its use within `ListenerImpl` and\n`ListenerOrchestrator`.\n\"\"\"\n\nos.makedirs('/workspace/source/docs', exist_ok=True)\nwith open('/workspace/source/docs/listener.md', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(content)\n\nprint(\"File written successfully\")\n\n# Verify line 27\nlines = content.split('\\n')\nprint(f\"Line 27: {lines[26]}\")\nEOF\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":808,"cache_read_input_tokens":69031,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":808},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QmDGCENxysf6vfJq1jinAP","session_id":"ad993b7d-d38f-4cff-844d-73e3581e179e","uuid":"79646bad-c768-40fe-9df7-bda313de6b5a"}
